prompt: An account report is correct when each order has one fill and one fee, but overstates both quantity and fees when either side has multiple child rows. Repair the query so independent one-to-many relationships are aggregated without a Cartesian multiplication.

repo_files.report.sql:
SELECT
  orders.account_id,
  COALESCE(SUM(fills.quantity), 0) AS total_quantity,
  COALESCE(SUM(fees.amount), 0) AS total_fees
FROM orders
LEFT JOIN fills ON fills.order_id = orders.id
LEFT JOIN fees ON fees.order_id = orders.id
GROUP BY orders.account_id
ORDER BY orders.account_id;
repo_files.tests/test_public.py:
import sqlite3
from pathlib import Path


def test_single_fill_and_fee_report():
    connection = sqlite3.connect(':memory:')
    connection.executescript(Path('schema.sql').read_text(encoding='utf-8'))
    connection.execute("INSERT INTO orders VALUES (1, 'acct')")
    connection.execute('INSERT INTO fills VALUES (1, 1, 3)')
    connection.execute('INSERT INTO fees VALUES (1, 1, 0.5)')
    row = connection.execute(Path('report.sql').read_text(encoding='utf-8')).fetchone()
    assert row == ('acct', 3, 0.5)
